phase1_subquery.description: Scan all 60M lineitem rows; accumulate SUM(l_quantity) per l_orderkey via 64-way scan-time partitioned aggregation; HAVING SUM > 300 → ~624 qualifying orderkeys stored in 7.5MB bitset
phase1_subquery.slot: int32_t key + int64_t sum_qty_int (l_quantity is integer-valued double, accumulate as int64 via (int64_t)l_quantity)
phase1_subquery.CRITICAL_C36_note: p1_part and p1_hash MUST use orthogonal bit ranges of same hash. Both using low bits → catastrophic probe chains → timeout
phase1_subquery.scatter_phase: 64 threads each route (l_orderkey, l_quantity_int) into 64 per-thread partition buffers; barrier after scan
phase1_subquery.aggregate_phase: thread t drains all 64 threads' buffers for partition t into one 524288-slot hash table; HAVING filter: collect keys where sum_qty > 300
phase1_subquery.output_structure: bitset uint8_t[7500001] (60M bits = 7.5MB); set bit: bitmap[ok >> 3] |= (1u << (ok & 7)); test: bitmap[ok >> 3] & (1u << (ok & 7))

phase2_main.description: Filter orders + lineitem by 7.5MB bitset; aggregate SUM(l_quantity) per qualifying order; sort 624 results; emit top 100
phase2_main.orders_phase: parallel scan 15M orders rows; for each row test orderkey_bitmap[o_orderkey >> 3] & (1u << (o_orderkey & 7)); collect qualifying (o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice) into flat result array (~624 entries)
phase2_main.lineitem_phase: parallel scan 60M lineitem rows; bitmap filter l_orderkey → probe 2048-slot hash map keyed by o_orderkey; accumulate SUM(l_quantity) as double into hash map slot
phase2_main.cname_reconstruction: sprintf(buf, 'Customer#%09d', c_custkey) — c_name NOT in binary; no customer_pk_hash lookup needed

join_order.notes: Subquery: GROUP BY l_orderkey HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 300. Partitioned agg avoids T×G thread-local memory explosion (64×33M×12B >> LLC). Each partition's 524288-slot table = 6.3MB fits per-core LLC slice.

join_order.build: orders(624 qualifying rows collected in flat array)
join_order.probe: lineitem(4368 qualifying rows via bitmap filter)
join_order.notes: 2048-slot hash map keyed by o_orderkey; probe-aggregate fused: each matching lineitem row adds l_quantity directly into HT slot sum_qty. L1-resident.
c_name = sprintf('Customer#%09d', c_custkey). c_custkey already in qualifying orders array. No customer_pk_hash mmap needed.

data_structures.phase1_partition_buffers: per-thread vector<pair<int32_t,int32_t>>[64] for scatter phase; 60M rows / 64 partitions ≈ 937K rows/partition/thread buffer
data_structures.phase1_partition_hash_tables: 64 open-addressing hash maps, each P1_CAP=524288 slots, slot={int32_t key, int64_t sum_qty}, sentinel key=INT32_MIN (std::fill — C20), bounded probe C24
data_structures.orderkey_bitmap: uint8_t[7500001] = 7.5MB bitset; L3-resident; covers o_orderkey max ≈ 60M
data_structures.phase2_agg_map: compact open-addressing hash_map, cap=2048 slots, slot={int32_t o_orderkey, double sum_qty}, sentinel=INT32_MIN, 24KB L1-resident
data_structures.result_buffer: flat array of 624 structs {int32_t o_orderkey, int32_t c_custkey, int32_t o_orderdate, double o_totalprice, double sum_qty}; in-place std::sort

parallelism.phase1_scatter: 64 threads, each thread maintains 64 partition buffers; morsel-driven over lineitem rows; no synchronization during scatter
parallelism.phase1_aggregate: #pragma omp parallel for (64 threads, one partition per thread); each thread drains own partition buffers, builds 524288-slot HT, applies HAVING, writes qualifying keys to shared bitmap with atomic OR
parallelism.phase2_orders_scan: 64 threads morsel-driven over 15M orders rows; bitmap test per row; qualifying rows appended to shared vector with mutex or lock-free append
parallelism.phase2_lineitem_scan: 64 threads morsel-driven over 60M lineitem rows; bitmap test per row; probe 2048-slot (L1-resident) agg map with __atomic_fetch_add or CAS for sum_qty

indexes.reason: orders scanned directly with bitmap filter; all 624 qualifying rows collected in flat array from sequential scan; hash index probes unnecessary
c_name reconstructed via sprintf('Customer#%09d', c_custkey); no customer binary columns needed; index mmap skipped entirely

aggregation: two_phase: phase1=scan_time_partitioned_hash(64 partitions, 15M groups, slot=int32+int64, C36 orthogonal bits); phase2=compact_hash_map(2048 slots, 624 groups, probe_aggregate_fused)

correctness_rules.C36_CRITICAL: p1_part MUST use HIGH 6 bits: (key*2654435761u)>>26; p1_hash MUST use LOW bits: (key*2654435761u)&(P1_CAP-1). Both using low bits → all keys in partition cluster in same slots → catastrophic probe chains → 300s timeout
correctness_rules.C11: call gendb::init_date_tables() once at top of main() before any epoch_to_date_str calls
correctness_rules.C20: use std::fill<int32_t>(ht, ht+P1_CAP, INT32_MIN) for sentinel init — NEVER memset (memset(buf,0x80,n)→0x80808080≠INT32_MIN)
correctness_rules.C24: ALL hash table probes use bounded for-loop: for(uint32_t pr=0; pr<cap; pr++) — never unbounded while
correctness_rules.C31: printf("\"%s\",", cname) — always double-quote c_name in CSV output
correctness_rules.C33: sort comparator: if(a.o_totalprice != b.o_totalprice) return a.o_totalprice > b.o_totalprice; if(a.o_orderdate != b.o_orderdate) return a.o_orderdate < b.o_orderdate; return a.o_orderkey < b.o_orderkey; (o_orderkey as stable tiebreaker)
correctness_rules.P37: P1_CAP = next_power_of_2(15000000 / 64 * 2) = 524288. Do NOT use P1_CAP=65536 (undersized for sf10 — that was sf1 sizing). Initialize lazily per thread during aggregate phase to avoid 402MB init stall.
